identity.name: TeamPulse
identity.stage: Pre-launch — research phase complete, building toward first release

researchData.note: All data from user's research (n=24). Preserved exactly as provided — these drive design decisions.

keyFindings.designImplication: Search must be prominent. Wiki IA must be intuitive. Feed search should search content, not just titles.
Notification center must filter aggressively. Default to actionable notifications, batch low-signal ones.
Optimize for quick scanning. Key content must be visible within 10 seconds. No deep navigation to find today's updates.
Feed is the primary screen. Home should default to feed view, not a dashboard or overview.
Encourage wiki usage through cross-linking from feed, 'Related docs' suggestions, and wiki creation CTAs.

keyFindings.finding: Primary CTA is content creation — activation = first pulse within 48 hours
keyFindings.designImplication: New user onboarding must surface pulse creation prominently. Empty feed state should guide to first pulse.

keyFindings.finding: Friendly tone won over formal (83% preference)
keyFindings.designImplication: Content guide should use friendly, conversational tone. Humor in empty states. Never humor in errors.

primary.label: Team member (IC)
primary.description: Daily user who creates updates, reads the feed, and consults docs. 12-minute average sessions, feed-first behavior.
goals: Find what they need quickly (addresses R1)
frustrations: Too many clicks to create content

primarySections.name: Feed
primarySections.purpose: Team updates stream — primary content view

primarySections.name: Notifications
primarySections.purpose: Filtered alerts — actionable by default (per R2)

designDecisions: Search is elevated to primary nav (not just a search bar) because R1 shows 71% frustration with finding content
Feed is the default Home view because R4 shows 73% start there
Docs are cross-linked from feed to leverage R5 (2.4x retention for wiki users)

terminology.note: User explicitly tested these terms. They are user-researched, not inferred.

frames.name: Dashboard
frames.researchNote: Research suggests feed-first, not dashboard-first. This dashboard may be for lead view.

frames.name: 1. Feeds
frames.researchNote: Validates R4 — feed is already designed as primary screen

frames.name: Flow: Login → Onboarding → Dashboard
frames.researchNote: Onboarding should guide to first pulse (R6: activation = first pulse within 48hrs)

constraints: Humor in empty states, never in errors (user-specified)
Content creation is primary CTA (activation metric)
